notes: Specialty boundary is narrow to enterprise application identity integrations spanning SSO, provisioning, deprovisioning, mapping, and troubleshooting.
Authority sources are real public sources from Microsoft, Okta, OpenID Foundation, OASIS, RFC Editor, and NIST, each with refresh cadence, decay policy, and review date in the manifest.

The package includes specialist-owned scenarios for federation design, provisioning quality, mapping correctness, edge-case constraints, low-confidence escalation, adjacent-specialty rejection, and shared-meta-agent fallback.
The manifest defines bootstrap metadata, delegated request and response contracts, and fallback behavior for shared meta-agents required by issue #139.
Deployment package documents runtime model, tenant isolation, monitoring and logging expectations, rollback path, and deferred shared-meta-agent initialization.
Marketing readiness records buyer profile, packaging and subscription framing, proof points, and explicit go-to-market gaps without overstating unsupported claims.
